hero.title: The #1 Plugin Publisher
hero.subtitle: Production-ready Strapi V5 plugins built by ISOMORPH — the Strapi agency trusted by developers worldwide.
hero.description: Open-source Strapi V5 plugins with full TypeScript support, OWASP-audited security, and zero vendor lock-in. Trusted by 12,000+ developers. Built and maintained by the ISOMORPH team in France.

why.title: Why developers choose ISOMORPH plugins
why.intro: Every plugin we publish follows the same four non-negotiable pillars: security, performance, accessibility and documentation. No shortcuts, no exceptions.

security.title: Security-First
security.description: Every plugin is audited against OWASP Top 10. Rate limiting, Zod input validation, and HMAC webhook verification are built-in by default.

performance.title: Core Web Vitals Ready
performance.description: Optimized for Google's Core Web Vitals. Minimal bundle impact, lazy loading, efficient database queries, and no unnecessary re-renders.

support.title: Pro Support
support.description: Community tier on GitHub Issues. Pro and Enterprise plans include dedicated email support and SLA guarantees backed by the ISOMORPH team.

trust.title: Built to production standards
trust.description: ISOMORPH plugins follow the same engineering standards we apply to client projects: TypeScript strict mode, comprehensive test coverage, semantic HTML, and OWASP security audits before every release.
trust.item4: Zero breaking changes policy within major versions

plugins.title: Strapi V5 Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Production-ready open-source plugins for Strapi V5. Each plugin is TypeScript-first, OWASP-audited, and maintained long-term by the ISOMORPH team.
plugins.intro: We build the Strapi plugins that the ecosystem was missing. Every release goes through security review, performance benchmarking, and accessibility testing before it hits npm.

comments.name: strapi-plugin-comments
comments.tagline: The most complete comment system plugin for Strapi V5
comments.description: Add a production-grade comment system to your Strapi V5 backend in minutes. Supports unlimited nested threads, full moderation workflow, email notifications, reactions, webhooks, and multiple authentication strategies. Used by 12,000+ developers.
comments.shortDescription: Production-grade comment system with moderation, nested threads, multi-auth, webhooks and reactions.
comments.seoIntro: strapi-plugin-comments is the leading open-source plugin for adding a full-featured comment system to any Strapi V5 project. Install via npm, configure once, and get a complete REST and GraphQL API for your frontend — whether you use Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, or any other framework.

whyUse.title: Why use strapi-plugin-comments?
whyUse.description: Building a comment system from scratch on Strapi takes weeks. Authentication logic, nested thread traversal, moderation queues, spam filtering, notifications — each component adds complexity. strapi-plugin-comments bundles all of this into a single, well-maintained package that installs in under 5 minutes.
points: Actively maintained — last release < 30 days ago
Zero external SaaS dependencies — runs entirely on your infrastructure
Full source code on GitHub under MIT license

blog.title: Blog Comments
blog.description: Add threaded comments to your headless blog. Works with Strapi's content types out of the box.

community.title: Community Platforms
community.description: Build discussion forums, Q&A boards, or community feeds with nested threads and user reactions.

ecommerce.title: Product Reviews
ecommerce.description: Add customer reviews and ratings to e-commerce products powered by Strapi.

features.title: Everything you need for production
features.subtitle: 18 features out of the box — from basic threaded comments to enterprise-grade audit logs and SSO.

nested.title: Nested Comments
nested.description: Unlimited depth thread support with parent-child relationships. Efficiently traversed with recursive queries.

moderation.title: Full Moderation Suite
moderation.description: Approve, reject, or flag comments directly from the Strapi admin panel. Bulk actions and moderation queue included.

auth.title: Multi-Auth Support
auth.description: JWT, API Key, and anonymous commenting with granular, role-based permissions via Strapi RBAC.

notifications.title: Email Notifications
notifications.description: Automatic email notifications for replies, moderation alerts, and new comment events. Configurable templates.

reactions.title: Emoji Reactions
reactions.description: Configurable emoji reactions on comments. Supports custom reaction sets defined in the admin panel.

search.title: Full-Text Search
search.description: Search across all comments using Strapi's native filters. No additional search engine required.

pagination.title: Cursor & Offset Pagination
pagination.description: Efficient cursor-based and offset pagination for large comment threads. No performance degradation at scale.

spam.title: Spam Protection
spam.description: Built-in rate limiting per IP and user. Optional Akismet integration for ML-based spam detection.

reports.title: User Reports
reports.description: Let users flag inappropriate content. Reports surface in the admin moderation queue for review.

export.title: CSV Export
export.description: Export all comments or filtered subsets to CSV directly from the Strapi admin panel.

webhooks.title: HMAC-Signed Webhooks
webhooks.description: Secure webhooks on comment creation, approval, and deletion. HMAC signature verification prevents spoofing.

i18n.title: i18n Ready
i18n.description: Full compatibility with Strapi's official i18n plugin. Comment threads are locale-aware.

typescript.title: 100% TypeScript
typescript.description: Full TypeScript with complete type definitions exported. Works seamlessly with strict mode projects.

rest.title: REST API
rest.description: Standard RESTful endpoints with full OpenAPI 3.0 documentation. Easy to integrate with any frontend.

graphql.title: GraphQL Support
graphql.description: Optional GraphQL resolver extension for Strapi's GraphQL plugin. Query comments with your existing GraphQL setup.

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.description: Complete immutable audit trail for all moderation actions. Required for compliance and GDPR accountability.

sso.title: Enterprise SSO
sso.description: SSO integration via Strapi Enterprise authentication. For large teams with centralized identity providers.

customFields.title: Custom Fields
customFields.description: Extend the comment model with your own fields. Add ratings, categories, or any custom metadata.

howItWorks.title: Up and running in 5 minutes
howItWorks.subtitle: Install once. Configure in the admin. Call the API from your frontend.

install.title: Install via npm
install.description: Run npm install strapi-plugin-comments and register the plugin in your Strapi config/plugins.js file.

configure.title: Configure in the admin
configure.description: Set permissions, moderation rules, notification settings, and allowed reactions directly in the Strapi admin panel.

use.title: Call the API
use.description: Use the REST or GraphQL API from your frontend. Complete documentation with examples for Next.js, Nuxt, and SvelteKit.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything developers ask before installing strapi-plugin-comments.

strapi4.question: Does strapi-plugin-comments support Strapi V4?
strapi4.answer: Version 2.x targets Strapi V5 exclusively. For Strapi V4, install version 1.x of the plugin (still maintained for critical security fixes).

frontend.question: Does it work with Next.js, Nuxt, or SvelteKit?
frontend.answer: Yes. The plugin exposes a standard REST API and an optional GraphQL extension. It is completely framework-agnostic on the frontend — any HTTP client works.

migrate.question: Can I upgrade from Community to Pro without losing data?
migrate.answer: Yes. Upgrading only activates additional features via license key — no schema changes, no data migration needed. All existing comments are preserved.

selfhost.question: Is the plugin self-hosted?
selfhost.answer: Yes. Community, Pro, and Enterprise tiers are all 100% self-hosted. The plugin runs entirely on your Strapi instance with no external SaaS dependency.

support.question: What support is included with each tier?
support.answer: Community: GitHub Issues with community responses. Pro: dedicated email support with 48h SLA. Enterprise: private Slack channel and 4h response SLA.

performance.question: How does the plugin perform at scale?
performance.answer: The plugin uses efficient SQL queries with proper indexing. Cursor-based pagination prevents n+1 issues. It has been tested with 500k+ comments without performance degradation.

gdpr.question: Is the plugin GDPR compliant?
gdpr.answer: The plugin stores no data outside your own database. All personal data stays on your infrastructure. The audit log and CSV export features support GDPR right-of-access and deletion requests.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free with the Community tier. Upgrade when you need production-grade features.
pricing.guarantee: All paid tiers include a 14-day money-back guarantee.

community.name: Community

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For large-scale deployments and compliance-sensitive projects.

about.title: About ISOMORPH
about.subtitle: We are the Strapi agency — building production-grade plugins and custom Strapi solutions for teams worldwide.
about.description: ISOMORPH is a web development agency founded by Florent Ducase, based in Paris and Toulon, France. We specialize in Strapi V5, Next.js App Router, and TypeScript-first development. Our open-source plugins are used by over 12,000 developers.

founder.title: Founded by Florent Ducase
founder.description: Florent is a senior full-stack developer with over 10 years of experience building web applications. He founded ISOMORPH to fill a gap in the Strapi ecosystem: production-grade plugins that meet the same engineering standards as enterprise software — security audits, comprehensive tests, proper documentation, and long-term maintenance.

expertise.title: Our technical expertise
expertise.description: Every project at ISOMORPH is built on a four-pillar foundation that is non-negotiable: RGAA 4.1 accessibility, RGESN 2024 eco-design, Core Web Vitals performance, and OWASP Top 10 security. These are not checklist items — they are engineering disciplines embedded in our workflow.

pillars.name: Accessibility — RGAA 4.1
pillars.description: Semantic HTML, WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios, keyboard navigation, and screen reader testing on every interface we deliver.

pillars.name: Eco-design — RGESN 2024
pillars.description: Pages under 1MB, WebP/AVIF images, lazy loading, minimal DOM nodes, and CSS-only animations. We measure what we ship.

pillars.name: Performance — Core Web Vitals
pillars.description: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1. Verified with Lighthouse and real-user data before every deployment.

pillars.name: Security — OWASP Top 10
pillars.description: CSP headers, server-side authentication, Zod input validation, rate limiting, and fail-closed defaults on every backend we build.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Making Strapi V5 accessible to every development team with professional-grade open-source plugins. We believe that production quality should not require enterprise budgets — so the Community tier is always free, always MIT, always maintained.

openSource.title: Open Source First
openSource.description: All our plugins are MIT licensed. The Community tier is always free and the source code is always public on GitHub.

quality.title: Production Quality
quality.description: TypeScript strict mode, comprehensive tests, OpenAPI documentation, and security audits — we do not ship shortcuts to production.

transparency.title: Full Transparency
transparency.description: Public roadmap, public changelogs, public GitHub issues. Every decision we make on plugins is visible and open for discussion.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: Whether you need help with a plugin, a custom Strapi implementation, or a full-stack project — we are here.
